Fici, S. 2017. A taxonomic revision of the genus Capparis (Capparaceae) in New Caledonia, New Zealand Journal of Botany 55: 407-423
Jacobs, M. 1960. Capparidaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 6: 61-105
Jacobs, M. 1964. The genus Crateva (Capparaceae). Blumea 12: 177-208
Jacobs, M. 1965. The genus Capparis (Capparaceae) from the Indus to the Pacific. Blumea 12: 385-541
Julius, A. 2022. ﻿Capparis (Capparaceae) in Peninsular Malaysia, including a new species and two new varieties. PhytoKeys 189: 99-127
Maurya, S & RK Coudhary. 2021. Bringing Capparis zeylanica (Capparaceae) out of chaos: nomenclatural updates and amended description. Phytotaxa 528 (2021) 263-268.

Su, J-X, W Wang, L-B Zhang & Z-D Chen. 2012. Phylogenetic placement of two enigmatic genera, Borthwickia and Stixis, based on molecular and pollen data, and the description of a new family of Brassicales, Borthwickiaceae. Taxon 61: 601-611

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1825) 53 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 81; Jacobs, Blumea 12 (1965) 440; Type: from Java. = Capparis tylophylla Spreng. , Syst. Veg., 16th Ed. 4 (1827) 204 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 213. = Capparis cumingii Merr. & Rolfe , PJS 3c (1908) 101; Type: Cuming 1234, Luzon: Albay. = Capparis turczaninowii Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1755. = Capparis mucronata Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1757.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Madura, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan. BOHOL, CORON, LUZON: Albay, Bataan, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Nueva Ecija, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, ?NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN.
species.notes: Low elevation dry thickets, secondary forests, sometimes on limestone-derived soil. 0-700(-1700)m.

species.citation: Lour. , Fl. Cochinch. (1790) 331 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 76; Jacobs, Blumea 12 (1965) 441.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, China, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, Myanmar, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. MINDANAO: Davao Oriental.
species.notes: Forest and forest edges, frequently in shade, seems to prefer moist places. 0-1750(-2000)m.

species.citation: Wight , Ill. Ind. Bot. (Madras) 1 (1839) 35 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 78; Jacobs, Blumea 12 (1965) 453. forma floribunda. = Capparis luzonensis Turcz., Bull. Soc. Imp. Naturalistes Moscou 27 (1854) 324. = Capparis luzonensis var. ampla Merr., EPFP 2 (1923) 212; Type: BS 8093 Ramos (syn); FB 11593 Curran (syn); FB 16745 Curran (syn); Adduru 260 (syn); Elmer 5883 (syn). = Crateva octandra Blanco, Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 400; Fl. Filip. ed. 2 (1845) 280; Fl. Filip. ed. 3, 2 (1878) 155. Distribution: Andaman Isls, India, Java, Moluccas, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. BABUYAN ISLS, DEBANGAN, LUZON: Benguet, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Quezon, Rizal. Notes: Low and medium elevation dry thickets and forests, on rocky sites, sometimes on coastal vegetation. Native.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, India, Java, Moluccas, Myanmar, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. BABUYAN ISLS, BANTAYAN (photos), DEBANGAN, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Benguet, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Mountain Province, NCR, Quezon, Rizal, MARINDUQUE, MINDORO.

species.citation: DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 248 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 77; Jacobs, Blumea 12 (1965) 459. = Capparis copelandii Elmer , LPB 2 (1910) 680 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 210. = Capparis oblongata Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 35 (1906) 15 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 212. = Capparis viridis Elmer , LPB 8 (1919) 3076.
species.distribution: Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madura, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi. ALABAT, GUIMARAS, HUNDRED ISLS (photos), LUZON: Bataan, Camarines Norte, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, PANAY (photos), POLILLO.

species.citation: Turcz. , Bull. Soc. Imp. Naturalistes Moscou 27 (1854) 323 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 211; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 79; Jacobs, Blumea 12 (1965) 463; Type: Lobb 467 (L, MO, iso), Philippines; locality in isotype sheets labeled (erroneously) as from Java. = Capparis ilocana Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 13 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 211; Type: BS 27120 Ramos. = Capparis littoralis Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 270 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 211; Type: FB 11111 Curran. = Capparis loheri Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 270 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 211. = Capparis palawanensis Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 304 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 212. = Capparis sepiaria var. acuta S.Vidal , Revis. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 47; Type: Vidal 24 (MA), Luzon: Camarines Sur, Sipocot.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Bataan, Batangas, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, Tarlac, Zambales, MINDANAO: Misamis Oriental (photos), MINDORO, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Dry rocky sites, coastal and bamboo thickets, also in primary forests. 0-600m.

species.citation: DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 247 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 215; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 212; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 85; Jacobs, Blumea 12 (1965) 466. = Capparis venosa Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 305 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 213. var. micracantha. Distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON, MINDANAO. Notes: N Luzon to Mindanao. Thickets, second-growth forests, etc. at low and medium elevation; common. Native.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Borneo, Cambodia, China, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BILIRAN, BORACAY (photos), BUSUANGA, BUYAYAO (photos), CULION, LUZON: Abra (photos), Ilocos Norte, La Union (photos), Pampanga (photos), Pangasinan, Quezon (photos), Rizal, MINDANAO: Surigao, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), TABLAS.

species.citation: DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 246 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 82; Jacobs, Blumea 12 (1965) 479. = Capparis cerasifolia A.Gray , U.S. Expl. Exped., Phan. (1854) 71 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 210. = Capparis lasiopoda Turcz. , Bull. Soc. Imp. Naturalistes Moscou 27 (1854) 323.
species.distribution: Borneo, China, Indochina, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madura, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. JOLO, LUZON: Albay, Cagayan, Ilocos Norte, La Union, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Davao, Lanao, Maguindanao del Norte, Surigao, Surigao del Norte, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur, PALAWAN, PUJADA, SULU ARCHIPELAGO.

species.citation: L. , Syst. Nat., 10th Ed. 2 (1759) 1071 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 215; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 212; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 79; Jacobs, Blumea 12 (1965) 466. = Capparis affinis Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 303 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 210. = Capparis emarginata C.Presl , Reliq. Haenk. 2 (1835) 85 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 211. var. sepiaria. Distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Borneo, China, India, Indochina, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Thailand. BABUYAN ISLS, CAGAYAN DE SULU, LUZON: Batangas, Ilocos Norte, La Union, Laguna, NCR, Pangasinan, Rizal, Zambales (photos), MINDANAO: Zamboanga, PALAWAN, QUEZON ISL, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, UBIAN. Native.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Borneo, China, India, Indochina, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Thailand. BABUYAN ISLS, CAGAYAN DE SULU, LUZON: Batangas, Ilocos Norte, La Union, Laguna, NCR, Pangasinan, Rizal, Zambales (photos), MINDANAO: Zamboanga, PALAWAN, QUEZON ISL, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, UBIAN.
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines; dry lowland or coastal thickets, bound to seasonal climatic conditions. Deciduous when fruiting. Sea level and lowland.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 503. ssp. cordifolia (Lam.) Fici, Phytotaxa 203 (2015) 28; ≡ Capparis cordifolia Lam., Encycl. (Lamarck) 1 (1785) 609; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 216; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 210. = Capparis spinosa var. mariana (Jacq.) K.Schum., Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 9 (1888) 201; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 89; ≡ Capparis mariana Jacq., Pl. Rar. Hort. Schoenbr. 1 (1797) 57. = Capparis sandwichiana DC., Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 1 (1824) 245. Distribution: Lesser Sunda Isls, New Caledonia, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls. BOHOL, LUZON. Notes: Occasionally cultivated in Manila, found spontaneous along the seashore in Bohol. 0-350m. Naturalized.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, Europe, Middle East, Nepal, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Pakistan, Philippines, Solomon Isls, W Asia. BOHOL, LUZON, MINDORO.

species.citation: Fici , Phytotaxa 267 (2016) 146; Type: Ridsdale SMHI 1792 (L, holo), Palawan: Narra, Victoria Peaks, Trident Mining Company concession area, 21-May-1984.

L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1762) 720 ; Jacobs, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1960) 87; Jacobs, Blumea 12 (1965) 505; Maurya & Choudhary, Phytotaxa 528 (2021) 263. = Capparis horrida L.f. , Suppl. Pl. (1782) 264 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 215; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 211.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Thailand, Vietnam. BATANES, LUZON: Apayao, Bataan, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, MINDANAO: Zamboanga, MINDORO, PALAUI, PANAY, SABTANG (photos), SULU ARCHIPELAGO.
species.notes: Low and medium elevation dry thickets. 0-700(-1000)m.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 12 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 211; Type: BS 26980 Ramos (US, iso), Luzon: Abra, Mt Posuey, Feb-1917.
species.notes: Holotype destroyed in 1945, and extant isotypes are sterile. Possibly a deviant Capparis pubiflora according to Jacobs.
